LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUZANNE M. SMITH'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 425.16) AND JOINDER IN HELLER EHRMAN LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. CCP SECTION 425.16 APPLIES TO MALICIOUS PROSECUTION ACTIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO PERMIT A FINDING THAT THEY WILL LIKELY PREVAIL ON EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE PRIOR ACTION "WAS INTIATED BY OR AT THE DIRECTION OF THE DEFENDANT" WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANTS HELLER, EHRMAN, SMITH AND KEEGAN, AND DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE ACTION "WAS BROUGHT WITHOUT PROBABLE CAUSE" AND "INITIATED WITH MALICE" AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS INCLUDING COOK. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761

Defendant Heller, Ehrman, Llp Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Oct-22-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Heller Ehrman LLP's Special Motion To Strike Complaint (Code Civ. Proc. Section 425.16).
APPEARANCES: Anthony J. Poidmore, Esq. of Law Offices of Anthony J. Poidmore 916/787-1290 for pltfs & opposing parties; Timothy J. Halloran, Esq. & Christian Penn Foote, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney 788-1900 for defts David J. Cook, Robert J. Perkiss, Debra D. Lew, and Cook, Perkiss & Lew; William J. Keegan, Esq. of The Keegan Law Firm 415/493-9393 for defts William J. Keegan and The Keegan Law Firm; Pamela Phillips, Esq. of Howard Rice Nemerovski Canady Falk & Rabkin 434-1600 for defts Suzanne M. Smith, and Heller Ehrman LLP.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. CCP SECTION 425.16 APPLIES TO MALICIOUS PROSECUTION ACTIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO PERMIT A FINDING THAT THEY WILL LIKELY PREVAIL ON EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE PRIOR ACTION "WAS INTIATED BY OR AT THE DIRECTION OF THE DEFENDANT" WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANTS HELLER, EHRMAN, SMITH AND KEEGAN, AND DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE ACTION "WAS BROUGHT WITHOUT PROBABLE CAUSE" AND "INITIATED WITH MALICE" AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS INCLUDING COOK. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/JKS/LB) JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761
